assertion_results.text: Tasks target both 'trustify-backend' and 'trustify-ui' repositories with at least one task per repo
assertion_results.evidence: Tasks 1, 2, 6 have '## Repository\ntrustify-backend'; Tasks 3, 4, 5 have '## Repository\ntrustify-ui'. Both repos have multiple tasks.

assertion_results.text: Backend tasks have lower task numbers than the frontend tasks that depend on them
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks are 1 and 2 (trustify-backend). Frontend tasks are 3, 4, 5 (trustify-ui). Task 3 (frontend) depends on Task 2 (backend): 'Depends on: Task 2 — Add SBOM comparison REST endpoint with integration tests'. Backend task numbers (1, 2) are lower than all frontend task numbers (3, 4, 5). Task 6 is backend but is a documentation task that depends on all others; no frontend task depends on it.

assertion_results.text: UI-facing frontend tasks (pages, components) reference Figma design context mentioning specific PatternFly components and visual specifications — API-layer frontend tasks (API types, client functions, hooks) are exempt from this requirement
assertion_results.evidence: Task 4 (SBOM comparison page, UI-facing) has extensive 'Figma design context (from figma-context.md):' section listing PatternFly components: Select (single, typeahead), ExpandableSection, Badge, Table (composable, sortable), SeverityBadge, EmptyState with CodeBranchIcon, Dropdown, Skeleton, plus badge colors per section and critical vulnerability row highlighting. Task 5 (route/list integration, UI-facing) references 'Figma design context (from figma-context.md):' for URL query params and bookmarking. Task 3 (API types, client function, hook) is API-layer and exempt — correctly omits Figma design context.

assertion_results.text: Cross-repo dependency ordering is enforced: either at least one frontend task explicitly depends on a backend task in its Dependencies section, or all tasks target a shared feature branch where backend tasks have lower task numbers than frontend tasks (implicit ordering via feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: Task 3 (trustify-ui) Dependencies section: 'Depends on: Task 2 — Add SBOM comparison REST endpoint with integration tests'. Task 2 is a trustify-backend task. This is an explicit cross-repo dependency from a frontend task to a backend task. Impact map also states: 'Cross-repo dependency ordering: Backend tasks (1-2) must complete before frontend tasks (3-5) that depend on the comparison endpoint.'

assertion_results.text: Frontend tasks reference paths from repo-frontend.md; backend tasks reference paths from repo-backend.md
assertion_results.evidence: Backend task 1 references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/mod.rs (from repo-backend.md). Backend task 2 references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs, tests/api/sbom.rs (from repo-backend.md). Frontend task 3 references src/api/models.ts, src/api/rest.ts, src/hooks/ (from repo-frontend.md). Frontend task 4 references src/pages/ structure, src/components/SeverityBadge.tsx, src/components/EmptyStateCard.tsx (from repo-frontend.md). Frontend task 5 references src/routes.tsx, src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx (from repo-frontend.md). No cross-repo path contamination.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All six task files start with '## Repository' followed by a single name: task-1 'trustify-backend', task-2 'trustify-backend', task-3 'trustify-ui', task-4 'trustify-ui', task-5 'trustify-ui', task-6 'trustify-backend'. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All six task files contain the required sections: '## Target Branch' (all set to 'main'), '## Description' (multi-paragraph descriptions), '## Acceptance Criteria' (checkbox lists), and '## Test Requirements' (checkbox lists). Verified in task-1-sbom-comparison-service.md through task-6-documentation.md.

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the corresponding mock repository structure manifests (repo-backend.md or repo-frontend.md), not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 Files to Modify: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs and modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/mod.rs — both exist in repo-backend.md tree. Task 2 Files to Modify: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs — exists in repo-backend.md. Task 3 Files to Modify: src/api/models.ts and src/api/rest.ts — both in repo-frontend.md. Task 5 Files to Modify: src/routes.tsx and src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx — both in repo-frontend.md. New files are created under directories that exist in the manifests (e.g., modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/, src/hooks/).

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every non-bookend, non-documentation task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance — bookend tasks (create-branch, merge-branch) and documentation tasks are exempt as they omit Implementation Notes by design
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 Implementation Notes reference: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/summary.rs (SbomSummary), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (SbomService), modules/fundamental/src/package/model/summary.rs (PackageSummary), entity/src/sbom_package.rs, common/src/db/query.rs, common/src/error.rs. Task 2 references: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/get.rs, list.rs, tests/api/sbom.rs. Task 3 references: src/api/models.ts, src/api/rest.ts, src/api/client.ts, src/hooks/useSbomById.ts. Task 4 references: src/pages/SbomDetailPage/, src/components/SeverityBadge.tsx, src/components/EmptyStateCard.tsx, src/hooks/useSboms.ts, src/utils/severityUtils.ts. Task 5 references: src/routes.tsx, src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx, src/components/FilterToolbar.tsx. Task 6 is documentation — exempt, no Implementation Notes.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section — either all set to 'main' (direct-to-main workflow) or bookend tasks set to 'main' with intermediate tasks set to the feature issue ID (feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: All six tasks have '## Target Branch' set to 'main'. Impact map confirms 'Selected mode: direct-to-main'. Consistent direct-to-main workflow with no bookend tasks.

assertion_results.text: For each task created, a description digest is produced — evidenced by a separate digest file (e.g., task-N-digest.md), a digest entry in the impact map, or a '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' marker in any output file. The digest must contain a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 digest files exist: task-1-digest.md (sha256-md:c0372119454e6d741ea462ad2738158172b9c8dcfd0fdf8349094eb56cd3412e), task-2-digest.md (sha256-md:75b0946e511e7b36e41ca5404ffd7c7760ec6bfcc1a0f779b97571f59f8d1574), task-3-digest.md (sha256-md:f871cab85931222a86dcfc1e220dbb2249b8837e66eafbcf1af0cfc634738ef8), task-4-digest.md (sha256-md:43789bf537fcc7cdd522cbe539fe06faa8e13a74d8d9e58f67592de32ac4bb7b), task-5-digest.md (sha256-md:912c0d774a0fb9709794cb9a7145f2578dd956672d84eee9121f53572748609c), task-6-digest.md (sha256-md:2ff28ed459cdade9f5345a7de4681a7e8593a2b53000d83d0b1e80862f8fe7f2). All are 64 lowercase hex chars with 'sha256-md:' prefix.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: All convention references use the prescribed format. Task 1: 'Applies: task creates `modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/compare.rs` matching the convention's `.rs` module file scope.' Task 2: 'Applies: task modifies `modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs` matching the convention's `.rs` endpoint file scope.' Task 3: 'Applies: task modifies `src/api/rest.ts` matching the convention's `.ts` API file scope.' Task 4: 'Applies: task creates `src/pages/SbomComparePage/SbomComparePage.tsx` matching the convention's `.tsx` page file scope.' Task 5: 'Applies: task modifies `src/routes.tsx` matching the convention's `.tsx` route file scope.' No inapplicable conventions listed with 'Not applicable' annotations. Backend tasks include only .rs-scoped conventions; frontend tasks only .ts/.tsx-scoped conventions.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), the plan documents that the Feature's priority is inherited by created tasks — evidenced by priority field values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the plan documents that priority inheritance is skipped (not propagated as null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: Feature TC-9003 has Priority: Critical. Impact map 'Inherited Field Propagation' section: 'Priority: Critical (inherited from Feature TC-9003; propagated to all tasks)'. Additional Fields JSON: '"priority": {"name": "Critical"}'. Summary-comment.md: 'Priority: Critical — inherited from Feature TC-9003 and propagated to all created tasks.'

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), the plan documents that fixVersions are inherited by created tasks — evidenced by fixVersion values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the plan documents that fixVersion inheritance is skipped
assertion_results.evidence: Feature TC-9003 has Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0. No Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md, so scope defaults to 'both'. Impact map: 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0 (inherited from Feature TC-9003; propagated to all tasks — fixVersion scope defaults to "both" since no Jira Field Defaults override is configured)'. Additional Fields JSON: '"fixVersions": [{"name": "RHTPA 1.5.0"}]'. Summary-comment.md: 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0 — inherited from Feature TC-9003 and propagated to all created tasks.'

assertion_results.text: A summary of inherited field propagation is produced — evidenced by a summary-comment.md file in the outputs directory, or a summary section in the impact map that includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: summary-comment.md exists in outputs directory with 'Inherited Field Propagation' section listing: 'Priority: Critical — inherited from Feature TC-9003 and propagated to all created tasks' and 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0 — inherited from Feature TC-9003 and propagated to all created tasks'. Impact map also has 'Inherited Field Propagation' section with the same data plus JSON representation.
